events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON. Need analyze gas meter image. Readings: O2 20.9% VOL (normal acceptable 19.5-23.5% typically). LEL 1% (below 10% acceptable). H2S 2 ppm (below OSHA PEL 10 ppm ceiling? acceptable). CO 170 ppm (above OSHA PEL 50 ppm TWA, also 1910.146 requires acceptable entry conditions: atmospheric concentration of any substance > PEL or IDLH; CO 170 ppm exceeds OSHA PEL 50 ppm and is dangerous; also IDLH for CO is 1200 ppm but entry not authorized because >PEL. Decision fail. Risk high. Work_stage planned. Component conditions maybe none. Percent complete 0. Value maybe 170 (CO reading). Findings: CO 170 ppm exceeds OSHA PEL 50 ppm; entry cannot be authorized. Actions: ventilate/reevaluate, identify CO source, retest before entry, ensure respiratory protection if needed. References: OSHA 1910.146(b)(def acceptable entry conditions), 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C), 1910.1000 Table Z-1 CO PEL 50 ppm TWA, 29 CFR 1910.146. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":false,"risk":"high","work_stage":"planned","component_conditions":[],"percent_complete":0,"value":170,"sound_source":"","confidence":1.0,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["CO reading is 170 ppm, which exceeds the OSHA PEL of 50 ppm and makes the space a permit-required confined space with unacceptable entry conditions","O2 (20.9 %VOL), LEL (1 %), and H2S (2 ppm) are within acceptable entry limits, but the CO reading controls the authorization decision"],"actions":["Do not authorize entry until CO is reduced to at or below the OSHA PEL/acceptable entry limit","Identify and eliminate/control the CO source","Ventilate and re-test the atmosphere at the required intervals and depths before entry","Ensure appropriate respiratory protection and rescue standby if entry becomes necessary after acceptable conditions are confirmed"],"rationale":"A permit-required confined space may only be entered when atmospheric conditions are within acceptable limits for all hazards. The 4-gas meter shows O2 20.9 %VOL, LEL 1 %, H2S 2 ppm, and CO 170 ppm. While oxygen, flammable gas, and hydrogen sulfide readings are acceptable, the carbon monoxide level of 170 ppm exceeds the OSHA PEL of 50 ppm (8-hour TWA) and therefore the space does not meet acceptable entry conditions under 1910.146. Entry must be prohibited until CO is controlled and re-testing confirms safe levels.","references":["29 CFR 1910.146 - Permit-required confined spaces","29 CFR 1910.146(b) - Acceptable entry conditions","29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) - Acceptable entry conditions verification","29 CFR 1910.1000 Table Z-1 - CO PEL 50 ppm (8-hour TWA)"]}
